behavior_summary: BELLOWS::look responds to an examine/U8P_BELLOWS::look action by preparing a short description string "bellows ", applying a processing/recipient exclusion so the bark is targeted, and invoking Item::bark to present that text to the examining actor/process. It then cleans up and suspends/returns.

BELLOWS::use toggles the bellows' visible state with a short animation: it queries the current frame (Item::getFrame) and chooses one of two 6-frame sequences (0..5 contraction or 6..11 expansion). For each frame in the chosen sequence it sets the frame (Item::setFrame), spawns a FREE::1C19 helper/effect with the invoking pid, suspends to allow timing between frames, and at the middle frame plays a sound (playSFX). The sequence results in a visual toggle between compressed and expanded bellows states.
notable_control_flow: BELLOWS::look: linear sequence — build text -> set processing/info exclusion -> call Item::bark(item, "bellows ") -> free temp string -> implication/pop housekeeping -> suspend/return.

BELLOWS::use: conditional branch on Item::getFrame(item): if frame < 6 then iterate frames 0..5 else iterate frames 6..11. Each iteration: Item::setFrame(item, frame) -> spawn FREE::1C19(pid, 0) -> suspend; at midpoint frame (2 or 8) also call playSFX(0x64, 0x10).
open_questions: What exact visual or gameplay effect does FREE::1C19 produce when spawned each frame (likely a short-lived visual or cleanup helper)?
What recipient filtering/exclusion semantics are applied in U8P_BELLOWS::look (which processes are excluded/included by the set-info/process-exclude calls)?
What is the exact suspend duration between frames (the disassembly shows suspend but not the timing constant)?
Is the sound id 0x64 mapped to a named sound resource in the game data, and does param 0x10 indicate volume or channel?

main_function.name: U8P_BELLOWS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_BELLOWS::look' (examine) action on the bellows item by announcing a short description ('bellows ') to the examining actor/process, applying recipient filtering to target the bark appropriately.
entry_conditions: Triggered when an actor/player examines or 'looks' at the bellows item (examine/U8P_BELLOWS::look verb).
Invoked with the item/process context (inferred item reference and caller pid).

function_map.name: U8P_BELLOWS::look
function_map.role: Respond to examine/U8P_BELLOWS::look verb by barking the description and applying recipient/process exclusion.

function_map.name: U8P_BELLOWS::use
function_map.role: Respond to use verb by animating the bellows between two visual states (frames 0..5 vs 6..11), spawning per-frame helpers and playing a midpoint sound.

external_calls.purpose: Display/announce the short description text ('bellows ') to the examining actor/process; the call is performed after setting processing/exclusion info so recipients are filtered.
Obtain the item's current visual frame to decide which 6-frame animation sequence to run (contraction vs expansion).
Update the item's visual frame each iteration to animate the bellows.
Create a short-lived helper/effect process tied to the animation frame (likely visual/cleanup) with the invoking pid and argument 0.
Play a sound cue at the midpoint of the animation (sound id 0x64, param 0x10) to emphasize the bellows action.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: BELLOWS::look responds to an examine/U8P_BELLOWS::look action by preparing a short description string "bellows ", applying a processing/recipient exclusion so the bark is targeted, and invoking Item::bark to present that text to the examining actor/process. It then cleans up and suspends/returns.
